chatgpt: Still my default for the questions I'd otherwise just sit with.
claude: The one I trust most when the stakes of being wrong are high.
cursor: The only AI tool that's made me a better programmer, not just a faster one.
github-copilot: Indispensable for the boring bits — get out of its way for the rest.
perplexity: What Google used to be in 2008, before the SEO sludge.
midjourney: When the brief is vague, it's a partner. When it's specific, fight it.
adobe-firefly: The version of generative AI you can show your legal team without flinching.
elevenlabs: Unsettlingly good, which is exactly the problem.
notion-ai: I keep enabling it, keep forgetting it exists, keep disabling it again.
gemini: Powerful, but constantly explaining itself when I didn't ask.
harvey-ai: If you're a lawyer, this is the AI conversation. If not, ignore it.
khanmigo: What I wish I'd had at fourteen, used badly.
openevidence: Removes the hour I used to spend on PubMed before forming a hypothesis.
alphafold: Changed what's tractable. Changed what's interesting. Most things still aren't either.
suno: Will write you a B-side. Won't write you a song you needed to make.
runway-ml: The future of video, currently held together with duct tape.
jasper: Built for a marketing team that needs to feed a content calendar; almost nobody else.
gong: The transcripts are great. The 'insights' are mostly the obvious thing wearing a hat.
notebooklm: I keep coming back. The audio overviews are uncannily good for long PDFs.
n8n: Worth learning if you've ever opened Zapier and felt cornered.
